Book

[Full details and actions for Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision: Explore Generative AI and Large Language Models with Hugging Face, ChatGPT, GPT-4V, and DALL-E 3](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/3376274)

Topics covered include the following:

- Business trends: Global changes in industries, overview of tech trends, Natural Language Processing evolution
- Sector innovations: Innovations in various sectors, NLP, word embedding, transformer architecture
- Digital products: Digitisation, products and the customer journey, architecture and use of BERT, architecture, and use of RoBERTa, pretrained language models and fine tuning
- Smart products and services: AI as a service, NLP theme continued, downstream NLP tasks with transformers, pretraining and fine tuning, methods of evaluating natural language understanding, GLUE, SuperGLUE
- Technology as a service: Machine translation, using transformers for translation, evaluating machine translations, BLEU, Google translate, Google TRAX, foundation models
- AI opportunities: The platform business model and sharing economy, GPT engines, few shot and zero shot classifications, GPT 3, GPT 4
- Enabling technologies: The data science stack, the expansion of transformer models, use of transformer models for image processing tasks, DALL-E
- Ethics and NLP futures: Ethics consideration, possibility of misuse, transformer driven co-pilots, prompt engineering, task specific transformers, future job roles

This module covers the following four learning outcomes:

MLO1 - A critical knowledge and understanding of AI and data processing theories and methodologies. (K1, K17, K24, B8)

MLO2 - Ability to apply AI methods and knowledge to a complex business challenge or project. (K4, K5, K6, K8 K21, S3, S6)

MLO3 - Ability to evaluate your AI skills and learning from innovation and assess performance and metrics of AI tools. (S5, S25, S26)

MLO4 - Reflect on the consequences of technological innovation and the impacts on society, environment, and political agendas (ethics, industrial strategy, sustainability, society, etc.). (K9, K11, K12, K29, B3, B4, B8)

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/what-will-you-learn-in-this-module-1738320623821/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/what-will-you-learn-in-this-module-1738320623821/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp)>

Knowledge

- K1: How to use AI and machine learning methodologies such as data-mining, supervised/unsupervised machine learning, Natural Language Processing, and machine vision to meet business objectives
- K4: How to extract data from systems and link data from multiple systems to meet business objectives.
- K5: How to design and deploy effective techniques of data analysis and research to meet the needs of the business and customers.
- K6: How data products can be delivered to engage the customer, organise information, or solve a business problem using a range of methodologies, including iterative and incremental development and project management approaches.
- K8: How to interpret organisational policies, standards, and guidelines in relation to AI and data.
- K9: The current or future legal, ethical, professional, and regulatory frameworks which affect the development, launch, and ongoing delivery and iteration of data products and services..
- K11: The roles and impact of AI, data science, and data engineering in industry and society.
- K12: The wider social context of AI, data science, and related technologies, to assess business impact of current ethical issues such as workplace automation and misuse of data.
- K17: How to identify current industry trends across AI and data science and how to apply these .
- K21: How AI and data science techniques support and enhance the work of other members of the team.
- K24: Sources of error and bias, including how they may be affected by choice of dataset and methodologies applied.
- K29: The need for accessibility for all users and diversity of user needs.

Skills

- S3: Critically evaluating arguments, assumptions, abstract concepts, and data (that may be incomplete) to make recommendations and enable a business solution or range of solutions to be achieved.
- S5: Manage expectations and present user research insight, proposed solutions, and/or test findings to clients and stakeholders.
- S6: Provide direction and technical guidance for the business with regard to AI and data science opportunities.
- S25: Select and use programming languages and tools and follow appropriate software development practices.
- S26: Select and apply the most effective/appropriate AI and data science techniques to solve complex business problems.

Behaviours

- B3: Acts with integrity with respect to ethical, legal, and regulatory factors, ensuring the protection of personal data, safety, and security.
- B4: Initiative and personal responsibility to overcome challenges and take ownership for business solutions.
- B8: Maintains awareness of trends and innovations in the subject area, utilising a range of academic literature, online sources, community interaction, conference attendance, and other methods which can deliver business value.

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/ksb-mapping-standard-1-0-st0763-1738321203732/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/ksb-mapping-standard-1-0-st0763-1738321203732/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp)>

Recommended reading list for AI and Digital Innovation

The main texts for this module are, they are all available at VLeBooks:

[Marr, B. (2022) Business trends in practice: the 25+ trends that are redefining organizations](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166)

[Marr, B. (2020) The intelligence revolution: transforming your business with AI](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2059863)

[Rothman, D. (2021) Transformers for Natural Language Processing](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2182859)

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/your-reading-list-1738322520153/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/module-overview-for-ai-and-digital-innovation-ai-1698/your-reading-list-1738322520153/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp)>

# Books

Innovation - Business focus:

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapters 1 & 2)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Innovation – NLP focus:

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Preface) – available via Kortext

From <[https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-1-introduction-to-innovation-in-ai-1698/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-1-introduction-to-innovation-in-ai-1698/?context_id=15568&context_resource=lp)>

# Notes

Factors shaping organisations

1. Changing relationship with the planet
2. Shifting economic and political power
3. Divergence and polarisation. e.g. Bexit or China's internet firewall
4. Shifting demographics. e.g. aging population, race etc.
5. Sociocultural and workplace shifts. e.g. home working. Worklife balance, mental health, wellbeing, etc.

Environmentally friendly, recycle, energy efficient buildings, etc.

Brexit lots of work and changes.

Natural wastage has lead to reduction in staff count.

No more homeworking. Services for mental health and wellbeing.

Influential trends

1. Ubiquitous computing - Moore's Law, cloud can deal with lots
2. Connected and smart everything - IoT, potential security risk
3. Datafication of the world - Data lakes and rivers. DNA storage in the future.
4. Artificial intelligence - Robotic Process Automation(RPA), Natural Language Generation(NLG), Natural Language Processing(MLP), machine vision, machine learning, deep learning.
5. Extended reality(XR) Virtual Reality(VR), augmented reality(AR) and Mixed reality(MR) allows interactiosn between digital and physical spaces.

Tech Trends

1. Digital trust - Blockchain and Distributed Ledger Technology(DLT)
2. 3D printing - Reduced costs, greater personalisation, low wastage.
3. Gene editing and synthetic biology. AI can help identify what DNA to change.
4. Nanotechnology and material science. Bend stuff, better solar power
5. New energy solutions. Wind, solar, green hydrogen, and maybe nuclear fusion in the future.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

1900s. Derdinand de Saussure saw language as a system.

1950 Turning - Turning test means they can think

1952 Hodgkin-Huxley show the brain used neurons to form an electrical network.

1957 Chomsky published Syntactic Structures. Phase-Structure Grammar which converts natural language sentences into a format for computers.

John McCarthy release LISP(Locator/Identifier Separation Protocol) suitable for NLP.

1964 Wezenbaum produced ELIZA imitate a psychiatrist using reflection techniques.

1964 US National Research Council(NRC) created Automatic Language Processing Advisory Committee(ALPAC) - NLP was beyond the reach of current technology

Was a mixture of linguistics and stats, but now moving over to pure statistics.

1997 Recurrent neural net(RNN)

2007 RNN used for voice and text processing

2000 Yoshuo Bengio A Neural Probabilistic Language Model. Feed forward architecture that takes input vector representations

![](data:image/png;base64,)

2010 Transformers are parrallelissible and not sequence dependent.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Further reading

This further reading is optional.

0:30, pp.139-172.

Chowdhary, K. and Chowdhary, K.R., (2020). Natural language processing. Fundamentals of artificial intelligence, pp.603-649.

Bibliography

[Bengio, Y., Ducharme, R. and Vincent, P.(2000) A neural probabilistic language model. Advances in neural information processing systems, 13](https://www.jmlr.org/papers/volume3/bengio03a/bengio03a.pdf). (jmlr.org)

[Foote K., D. (2023) A Brief History of Natural Language Processing, Web Article, Dataversity](https://www.dataversity.net/a-brief-history-of-natural-language-processing-nlp/).

[Grygoryev, N.T. (2023) What are Transformers in NLP: Benefits and Drawbacks, blog, pangeanic](https://blog.pangeanic.com/what-are-transformers-in-nlp) (pangeanic.com)

[Nzotta, C. (2003), A quick history of Natural Language Processing, aveni](https://aveni.ai/blog/history-of-natural-language-processing/))
